aids.title: MUSIC - Distributed System Characteristics
aids.content:
**M**ultiple nodes
**U**nified appearance
**S**hared goal
**I**ndependent operation
**C**ommunication via messages

aids.title: ALARM-FC - Types of Transparency
aids.content:
**A**ccess - hide data format differences
**L**ocation - hide where resources are
**A**ccess - (covered above)
**R**eplication - hide copies
**M**igration - hide movement
**F**ailure - hide crashes
**C**oncurrency - hide sharing
aids.forRemembering: Six types of transparency in distributed systems

aids.title: The Restaurant Chain Analogy
aids.content:
Think of a **restaurant chain** like McDonald's:

- Multiple locations (nodes) across cities
- Same menu everywhere (unified interface)
- Local kitchen prepares food (independent processing)
- Central inventory system (coordination)
- Customer doesn't care which branch made the burger (transparency)
- If one branch closes, others continue (fault tolerance)

This is exactly how distributed systems work!

aids.title: PUNCH - Distributed System Challenges
aids.content:
**P**artial failures
**U**nreliable network
**N**o global clock
**C**oncurrency issues
**H**eterogeneity

Remember: Distributed systems pack a PUNCH of challenges!
aids.forRemembering: Five major challenges in distributed systems

aids.title: Distributed Systems Rhyme
aids.content:
Many computers, working as one,
Passing messages till the job is done.
No shared memory, just the net,
A single system is what users get!

aids.title: Centralized vs Distributed
aids.content:
**Centralized:** One big tree with all branches
[Single Point of Failure]

**Distributed:** Forest of many trees
[If one tree falls, forest survives]

Scalability: Can't make tree taller infinitely
vs. Can always plant more trees!
